assertions.title: Recovery is visible in the Web UI after cleanup

steps.action: Open the target company issue list at /STAA/issues before unblock and inspect STAA-1.
steps.expected: Before recovery, determine whether the board presents STAA-1 as live/actively running stale work.
steps.observed: The board already rendered STAA-1 with a visible 'Recovered' badge and no live-run indicator, even though the pre-unblock issue API still reported status=in_progress/projectedStatus=in_progress with checkoutRunId set and active-run null.

steps.action: POST /api/orchestrator/issues/5987492a-ac80-4f24-8e35-db99d0c6d69b/unblock and re-read the issue/live-run APIs.
steps.expected: Manual unblock should clear stale ownership and converge the issue into a non-live follow-up state.
steps.observed: The unblock response returned issue.status='todo', and the follow-up issue read showed projectedStatus='todo', checkoutRunId/executionRunId null, active-run null, and company live-runs [].

steps.action: Refresh /STAA/issues after unblock and inspect STAA-1 again.
steps.expected: The Web UI should no longer present the issue as live/active and should show recovered follow-up work.
steps.observed: After refresh, STAA-1 remained visible in the issue list with the 'Recovered' badge and without any active/live run indicator, matching the recovered non-live API state.

evidence.consoleErrors: Observed non-blocking React console error on list/deep-link loads: 'Maximum update depth exceeded'. Validation remained possible on the company-scoped issue list.
evidence.network: Saved API/text evidence: hardening-regression/stale-visibility-rerun/VAL-STALE-005-issue-before.json, VAL-STALE-005-active-run-before.json, VAL-STALE-005-live-runs-before.json, VAL-STALE-005-unblock-response.json, VAL-STALE-005-issue-after.json, VAL-STALE-005-active-run-after.json, VAL-STALE-005-live-runs-after.json

frictions.description: Opening the unprefixed deep link first caused the app to resolve to /STA/issues/STAA-1 and render a blank broken page with a React 'Maximum update depth exceeded' error while stale company context synced.
frictions.resolution: Switched to the explicit company-scoped route /STAA/issues and validated the stale-recovery state from the issue list there.

summary: Tested 1 assertion (VAL-STALE-005): pass. After manual unblock, the board still showed STAA-1 as recovered/non-live rather than actively running, and the API converged to todo with no active/live runs.
